source: OpCo_Segmentation_Framework_v17.xlsx, Business Capabilities Matrix, Current-State Process Analysis, and segmentation transcripts (May 2026).

portfolio_context.process_similarity_note: Process mining of 85,070 calls found cosine similarity averaging 0.92 across OpCos - they vary far less in process than they claim; variance is mostly configuration and labels. Do not over-index on self-reported 'we're different'.
portfolio_context.acquisition_cliff: Post-2020 OpCos are markedly less digital (digitization composite 2.13 vs 3.8 pre-2020). Recent tuck-ins need onboarding simplicity, not feature depth.

dimensions.name: Size by revenue
dimensions.detail: Range $8.2M (Alpha) to $174.2M (Maxair). Bands at $15M/$30M/$60M/$100M. Primary axis.

dimensions.name: Headcount / field-tech count & revenue-per-tech
dimensions.detail: Median ~$432K revenue per field tech; median field-to-back-office ratio 4.1:1.

dimensions.name: Service vs. project / product mix
dimensions.detail: T&M service, PM contracts, and project/construction work. The definition of a 'project' varies by OpCo.

dimensions.name: Residential vs. commercial vs. industrial
dimensions.detail: Only 4 of 57 OpCos are >=5% residential (Gentry 61%, ENCON 51%, Comfort Flow 44%, Air Force One 10%). Residential is a specialty minority pattern.

dimensions.name: Trade diversification (Breadth 1-5)
dimensions.detail: 11 single-trade, 28 HVAC+1, only 4 at breadth-4. Skews narrow.

dimensions.name: Data/process maturity (digitization composite)
dimensions.detail: Avg of CRM, FSM/Mobile, Reporting, Asset-Data scores (1-5). Data readiness is a distinct lens.

dimensions.name: Talent / back-office capability
dimensions.detail: Ranges from staffed finance teams to 'an 18-year-old kid who shows up after school'. A sequencing gate.

dimensions.name: Union vs. non-union
dimensions.detail: First-class constraint. Sharing an asset across a union/non-union line can trigger unionization - hard lines in the sand; absolute entity isolation required.

dimensions.name: Geography / region (incl. Canada)
dimensions.detail: Southeast, Midwest & West, Northeast, Corporate cuts. Canada (e.g., Naylor, Oakville ON) referenced as an operating-model benchmark.

archetypes.name: Large, sophisticated, project-heavy multi-division
archetypes.characteristics: 85-90% T&M with on-the-fly scope change; internal rental cost centers; cradle-to-grave PMs paid on job margin; multi-state payroll; per-job rate overrides; GMP/progress billing.
needs: Project Execution (WIP, forecast updates, T&M conversion)

archetypes.name: Large, process-mature service/controls leader
archetypes.characteristics: Mature, opinionated, incumbent-anchored (often WennSoft). Can absorb governance and standardization. Pilot/migration anchor.

archetypes.name: Strategic / National Accounts
archetypes.characteristics: National customers, partner network where SL may not own the shop, margin-stacking opacity, 'one neck to choke', flying blind on SLAs/safety.

archetypes.name: Residential / white-glove specialty
archetypes.characteristics: High-touch dispatch, live good/better/best flat-rate quoting on site, zone/ZIP pricing, real-time customer comms. Off the standard commercial pattern.
needs: In-field flat-rate good/better/best quoting tied to ERP

archetypes.name: Mid-size, low-maturity manual shops (the 44% plurality)
archetypes.characteristics: No FSM or paper field workflow; weak back office. Largest single group (25 of 57).

archetypes.name: Small, low-funded / newly tucked-in (<$15M)
archetypes.characteristics: 7 OpCos totaling $73.1M; may not be ready for advanced tech; low funded.

corporate_floor.note: Scalable architecture, Enterprise Visibility & Governance, and Regulatory/Labor compliance are GATES across ALL segments - minimum thresholds, not weighted trade-offs. Score corporate dimensions centrally; do not let local business users (who may not know SOX) score them.
